title: Carbon isotope descrimination - part I - trapline - v0.1
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fihk9","text":"Protocol for collecting gas for measurement of carbon isotope descrimination during photosynthesis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3crp0","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5brbn","text":"See the following references for more background:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"96gft","text":"H. Griffiths (1993) Photosynthesis and Production in a Changing Environment pp 181-1927","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":20,"length":55}],"data":[]},{"key":"95v7t","text":"Farquhar et al.  (1989) Annu. Rev. Plant Physiol. Plant Mol. Bioi. 1989. 40:503-37 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4412A97E3D574D228ED58FC59B1FF5F0","url":"https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-94-011-1566-7"}}}}
steps: Clamp leaf in cuvette, under a bright ambient light. Adjust H2OR so that Tleaf is around 25 o C. This will need adjusting when leaf is reaching stability in the cuvette. For tobacco a 4 week old plant prior to flowering is in the ideal state (health example below). Make sure not to stress the plant by introducing tension when clamping. Illuminate from above (at least 200µE), this makes sure the surrounding leaf tissue is photosynthesising to reduce chance of diffusion of metabolites influencing measurements, this is more important for homobaric leaves such as tobacco. It helps to let the plant acclimate in the lab for ~24h prior to measurement. Wait with measurements and CO 2 trapping until complete steady state has been reached. Assimilation rate (A) and steady state fluorescence do not change over time (10 min window) Stomatal conductance (gs) does not change over time (10 min window) This can take ~2h depending on the plant. Check regularly (~every 15 mins after the first half hour) Note that mesophyll conductance can be altered by a whole range of factors (cell size, leaf expansion, age of leaf etc etc) so care should be taken in performing comparisons and should try to keep to plants at a consistent developmental stage and growth conditions. 01:00:00
Turn on cold finger This will be used to cool down acetone to below -70oC as required for sublimination of frozen CO2 There is only an ON/OFF setting.

Place cold finger in dewar of acetone and wait to cool down (>-70oC), it should turn milky white Cover the top of the dewar with foli to prevent ice forming and falling into the acetone. Do not rest the finger on the bottom of the dewar otherwise an ice lump forms and it will stick 01:00:00
Check temperature of acetone is below -70oC
Fill two dewars 3/4 full with LN2 These will be used to condense gases for sample collection. This can be done whilst waiting for the acetone to cool down

Open glass valves and wait until vacuum gauge reads around 5.5 mbar on each prep line. Adjust by opening/closing pump valves if required. Provide support from below when opening/closing valve. Apply downward pressure while twisting to minimize the chance of introducing leaks (this is the most common source in the system). Adjust the vacuum pressure by opening the valve to the pump (red arrow). This ensures that approximately the same amount of gas is collected for reference and sample and ensures consistency between measurements
Check that both collection tubes are open
Check if leaf is still in stable state (both A and gs)
Match LI-6800 manually Check settings automatch OFF Fluorescence OFF

Set the following environmental variables in the LICOR LI-6800 %s %s Control temperature on block (Txchg), not leaf otherwise photosynthetic rate will start to oscillate due to constant adjustment of stomata. TLeaf should ideally be within +/- 0.03oC of 25oC as measurement is temperature sensitive. 25oC is the value that C3 photosynthetic calculations are parematerized for. You can manipulate with humidity or Txchg on reference

When plant has reached a steady state switch inlet gas to 2% oxygen. Use a flow controller to moderate gas use, setting flow to ~6. Change the constant value in LICOR 2% O2 is used to suppress photorespiration simplifying downstream calculations of carbon flux. Note this will change assimilation and photosynthesis values which will take time to stabilize again, you may witness oscillations in A and F which will dampen down over time. When there is no flow the value should be ~0.5 or less If the cylinder is running low order a replacement, always keep a backup cylinder to avoid running out of gas 01:00:00
Immerse both traps in LN2 (2 dewars) The distillation tube should ideally be covered close to the top
Close the glass valves, watch the vacuum drop to low 10-2 or high 10-3E. Twist perpendicular to the flow direction otherwise there can be leaks, check the pressure gauge
For each prep line (one after the other) make sure the collection tubes are open.

Remove acetone and liquid N dewars When the vacuum stabilizes all gas has been condensed again, this is the point when you can close tube and remove from LN2 Collect drips of acetone from distillation tube in beaker

Remove collection tube from prep line and leave to thaw in rack Put new (empty and open) collection tube in place and open pump valve Opening the pump valve cleans out the prep line and residual air in the new collection tube
Take fluorescence measurement (Fm'Fs)
Set timer and start logging every minute min for 5 min (LI-6800, autoprogram) 05:00:00
Close the pump valves.
In a single motion remove distillation tube from LN2 and submerge in cold acetone to subliminate gases The pressure in the prep line should rise as gas is released
Close collection tube

Quickly submerge the collection tube in LN2 to condense the released gas The pressure should fall again as gas is drawn down into the collection tube. By following this procedure it is possible to collect gases and remove the water vapour from samples which would interfere with measurements Collection tube should be dipped in LN2, covering at least the bottom 2cm
Close low O2 cylinder and adjust constant value in LICOR
creator: Steven J Burgess
